errors.excerpt: Let $f,g:A\to\mathbb{R}$, $c$ a cluster point of $A$, $\operatorname{FunctionLimit}(f,c,L)$, $\operatorname{FunctionLimit}(g,c,M)$. errors.title: Neighbourhood Form of the Limit
errors.message: Extracted theorem/definition box appears to contain more than one primary statement. Extraction was preserved, but the source should be split into separate boxes and re-extracted.
errors.excerpt: Let $f : A \to \mathbb{R}$ and let $c$ be a cluster point of $A$. The following are equivalent: \begin{enumerate}[label=(\roman*)] \item $\lim_{x \to c} f(x) = L$. \item For every $\varepsilon$-neighbourhood $V_\varepsilon(L)$ there exists a $\delta$-neighbourhood $V_\delta(c)$ such that $x \in V_\delta^*(c) \cap A \Rightarrow f(x) \in V_\varepsilon(L)$. \end{enumerate}

errors.title: Sequential Criterion for Function Limits

errors.excerpt: Let $f : A \to \mathbb{R}$ and let $c$ be a cluster point of $A$. The following are equivalent: \begin{enumerate}[label=(\alph*)] \item $\lim_{x \to c} f(x) = L$. \item For every $(x_n) \subseteq A \setminus \{c\}$ with $x_n \to c$, $\; f(x_n) \to L$. \end{enumerate}

errors.title: Bounded Above, Bounded Below, Bounded
errors.message: Extracted theorem/definition box appears to contain more than one primary statement. Extraction was preserved, but the source should be split into separate boxes and re-extracted.
errors.excerpt: Let $A\subseteq\mathbb{R}$, $f:A\to\mathbb{R}$. \begin{itemize} \item $f$ \textbf{bounded above}: $\exists M,\;\forall x\in A: f(x)\leq M$. \item $f$ \textbf{bounded below}: $\exists m,\;\forall x\in A: f(x)\geq m$. \item $\operatorname{BoundedOn}(f,A)$: $\exists B>0,\;\forall x\in A: |f(x)|\leq B$. \end{itemize}

errors.title: Supremum, Infimum, Maximum, Minimum of a Function
errors.message: Extracted theorem/definition box appears to contain more than one primary statement. Extraction was preserved, but the source should be split into separate boxes and re-extracted.
signals: definition_title_contains_multiple_concepts:Supremum, Infimum, Maximum, Minimum of a Function
errors.excerpt: Let $A\subseteq\mathbb{R}$, $f:A\to\mathbb{R}$. \begin{itemize} \item $\sup_{x\in A}f(x) := \sup f(A)$;\quad $\inf_{x\in A}f(x) := \inf f(A)$. \item $\operatorname{Maximum}(x^*,f(A))$: $x^*\in A$ and $f(x^*)=\sup f(A)$. \item $\operatorname{Minimum}(x_*,f(A))$: $x_*\in A$ and $f(x_*)=\inf f(A)$. \end{itemize}

errors.excerpt: Let $f:A\to\mathbb{R}$, $A\subseteq\mathbb{R}$ nonempty. \begin{itemize} \item \textbf{Increasing}: $x_1<x_2\Rightarrow f(x_1)\leq f(x_2)$. \item \textbf{Strictly increasing}: $x_1<x_2\Rightarrow f(x_1)<f(x_2)$. \item \textbf{Decreasing}: $x_1<x_2\Rightarrow f(x_1)\geq f(x_2)$. \item \textbf{Strictly decreasing}: $x_1<x_2\Rightarrow f(x_1)>f(x_2)$. \item $\operatorname{MonotoneFunction}(f,A)$: increasing or decrea...

errors.title: Adherent Point; Isolated Point
errors.message: Extracted theorem/definition box appears to contain more than one primary statement. Extraction was preserved, but the source should be split into separate boxes and re-extracted.
errors.excerpt: Let $X \subseteq \mathbb{R}$ and $x \in \mathbb{R}$. $x$ is an \textbf{adherent point} of $X$ if \[ \forall\varepsilon > 0,\; \exists\, y \in X :\; \operatorname{Within}(y, x, \varepsilon). \] A point $x \in X$ is an \textbf{isolated point} of $X$ if $\neg\,\operatorname{ClusterPoint}(x, X)$; equivalently, $\exists\varepsilon > 0 : V_\varepsilon(x) \cap X = \{x\}$.
